title: Venetian Automaton Workshop
description: The user operates a steampunk workshop where a mechanical gear system powers an enchanted Venetian mask that animates a puppet figure (Kira). By coupling gear speeds, mask energy channels, and puppet joint controls, the user must choreograph a carnival dance sequence while conserving a finite pool of mechanical energy across all three subsystems, with full undo/redo support.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing a steampunk automaton workshop simulation. Load venice_mask.glb, gears.glb, and kira.glb using GLTFLoader. Scene layout: place the gears model on the left as a mechanical power source, the venice_mask centered and floating above a pedestal as an energy conduit, and kira on the right as the animated puppet. Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,2,6) looking at origin, with OrbitControls. Add ambient light (0.4), a warm point light on the gears, a purple point light on the mask, and a spotlight on kira. Implement a conservation-of-energy system: a total energy pool of 100 units displayed as an HUD bar. The user distributes energy across three subsystems via sliders in an HTML panel: (1) Gear Speed (0-50 units) — controls gears rotation speed, (2) Mask Glow (0-50 units) — controls emissive intensity and a pulsing particle aura around the mask, (3) Puppet Motion (0-50 units) — controls kira's procedural sway animation amplitude and speed. The sum of all three sliders must never exceed 100 (conservation law). When one slider increases, others auto-reduce proportionally. Implement multi-input coupling: gear speed above 30 causes the mask to vibrate; mask glow above 25 makes kira's eyes glow; puppet motion above 35 causes gears to emit sparks (simple particle system). Add 6 preset buttons (Dance, Idle, Dramatic, Surge, Whisper, Custom) that set specific energy distributions. Implement full undo/redo: every slider change or preset click pushes state to a history stack. Undo (Ctrl+Z) and Redo (Ctrl+Y) buttons restore previous energy distributions with smooth tweened transitions (lerp over 0.5s). Add a 'Lock Ratio' toggle that lets the user scale all three values proportionally. The interaction flow requires 10-14 steps: adjust each slider, try presets, observe coupling effects, use lock-ratio mode, and undo/redo through states. Render with antialiasing enabled and a dark gradient background.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  gearRotationSpeed: number,         // current angular velocity of gears in radians per second
  maskEmissiveIntensity: number,         // emissive material intensity of the mask (0.0-3.0)
  maskParticleCount: number,         // number of active particles in the mask aura
  puppetSwayAmplitude: number,         // maximum displacement of kira's sway animation in units
  puppetAnimationSpeed: number,         // speed multiplier for kira's procedural motion
  totalEnergyPool: number,         // total available energy units (fixed at 100)
  energyAllocatedGears: number,         // energy units assigned to gear subsystem (0-50)
  energyAllocatedMask: number,         // energy units assigned to mask subsystem (0-50)
  energyAllocatedPuppet: number,         // energy units assigned to puppet subsystem (0-50)
  conservationLawViolated: boolean,         // true if sum of allocations exceeds totalEnergyPool
  danceSequenceActive: boolean,         // true when choreographed animation is running
  systemStabilized: boolean,         // true when all subsystems reach steady state for 5+ seconds
}
